name: Affinity on Georgia
notes: 9-story, 210-unit apartment community managed by Bozzuto. Built on the former National Institute of Dry Cleaning site; integrates renovation of two existing 1920s buildings. Developer: Foulger-Pratt; architect: The Preston Partnership. Delivered June 2024.

Concierge service is listed as an amenity, but resident reviews specifically note the concierge 'is not staffed all night.' Leasing office hours are weekday daytime only. Classified as Daytime only pending direct confirmation.
Fitness center with golf simulator and massage area per UrbanTurf. Resident reviews note broken and missing equipment; condition described as inadequate by some reviewers.
Mixed reviews: some residents praise staff responsiveness; others report filthy hallways and unresponsive management. Not enough consensus to confirm well-maintained.
ApartmentList lists patio/balcony as a standard unit amenity.
Air conditioning listed as an in-unit feature, consistent with individual unit climate control.
Studios (~640 sq ft), 1BR (708-836 sq ft), 1BR+Den (~860 sq ft), 2BR (1,029-1,228 sq ft), 2BR townhomes (1,179-1,228 sq ft).
Overall range $1,732-$3,151/mo per UrbanTurf; 2BR units $2,382-$2,757/mo per ApartmentList (June 2026).
https://www.apartmentlist.com/md/silver-spring/affinity-on-georgia
https://dc.urbanturf.com/pipeline/100/Affinity_on_Georgia
https://www.bozzuto.com/apartments-for-rent/md/silver-spring/affinity-on-georgia
https://www.apartmentratings.com/md/silver-spring/affinity-on-georgia_9199332346275146310/
https://silverspringdowntown.com/go/affinity-on-georgia

name: Alexander House at Elizabeth Square
notes: Multiple listing aggregators and an ApartmentRatings.com summary describe '24-hour concierge'; a lobby redesign with controlled access and on-site security is confirmed. Hours not explicitly stated on any property-managed page; 'Likely staffed' is the honest call absent first-party confirmation of 24h staffing
One resident review explicitly listed 'washer and dryer in the unit' as a key amenity; the Ascend Apartments listing also notes in-unit laundry. Listed as Yes but not confirmed across all floor plans
Resident reviews mention 'a very nice swimming pool with a lifeguard every day'; confirmed
Resident review and Ascend Apartments listing both confirm balconies as a unit feature
Ascend Apartments listing confirms a 'state-of-the-art fitness center'
Zumper shows 'not allowed'; other listing sources (Ascend) show 'cat friendly' and 'dog friendly'. Conflict between sources; verify directly with leasing office
No explicit mention of a package room or package lockers; building has controlled access and concierge who may handle packages but this is unconfirmed
No source specifies whether heat/AC is tenant-controlled by thermostat or switched seasonally by building management; unclear

ApartmentRatings.com has 101 reviews with mixed sentiment; some reviewers note helpful staff and updated lobby, others cite maintenance delays and management concerns; insufficient data to call this Yes or No

16-story high-rise; 305 units; built 1992; managed by Pratumco (property manager Alexis King); owned/operated in partnership with the Housing Opportunities Commission (HOC) of Montgomery County; approximately 40% affordable units; part of Elizabeth Square development alongside Elizabeth House III and Elizabeth House IV
(301) 587-4277 | alexis.king@pratumco.com
One month free rent on select homes (as of June 2026); Saturday open houses 10 AM-2 PM
(301) 781-7567 (Ascend listing)

name: Arrive Silver Spring
notes: Concierge desk confirmed; described in multiple sources as 24-hour service, but leasing office hours are Mon-Fri 9am-6pm, Sat 10am-5pm, Sun 12pm-5pm. No explicit documentation of 24-hour staffing found on official site.
In-unit washer/dryer in select/renovated units; some units may rely on shared laundry facilities
$25/month per pet, max 2 pets, no deposit required; breed restrictions apply (pit bulls, rottweilers, German shepherds, others)
Central A/C and forced air heat listed; treated as in-unit controlled
Reviews are mixed: some residents report clean hallways and responsive maintenance; others cite chipped floors, dirty elevators, and slow repair response times. Unclear overall.
891 total units (high-rise)
Trinity Property Consultants
Red Line Metro (Silver Spring station) approximately 2 blocks away

name: Atwell on Spring
address: 1260 Spring St (8787 Georgia Ave), Silver Spring, MD 20910
notes: Multiple sources including ApartmentList and search results describe '24-hour concierge.' Bozzuto marketing materials and resident reviews reference around-the-clock concierge services. One reviewer named a concierge staff member by name and praised the team.
The project is commonly described as '8787 Georgia Ave' (the block address) but the street address listed on leasing platforms is 1260 Spring St, Silver Spring, MD 20910.
Package room exists with concierge accepting deliveries; at least one reviewer noted difficulty with the double-entrance/package room setup, so the feature exists but has had reported friction.

ApartmentList explicitly lists dogs and cats as permitted. ApartmentSearch showed 'not allowed' but that site flagged the property as 'not in our system', so ApartmentList is the more reliable source.

ApartmentList lists 'air conditioning' as an in-unit feature; Bozzuto positions units as full luxury with modern appliances. HVAC control per-unit (not building-controlled) is consistent with the luxury positioning and 2024 construction vintage but not explicitly confirmed from a lease document or resident statement.
ApartmentRatings.com summarized reviewer characterization as 'well-maintained, peaceful community with excellent management' with responsive maintenance staff. Building opened 2023-2024.
2BR range $2,780-$4,296/mo per ApartmentList (current as of search date June 2026). 1BR+den ~$2,838/mo. Studios from ~$1,752/mo.
Official gallery is at atwellonspring.com/gallery and bozzuto.com gallery page; og:image could not be retrieved due to 403 blocks on both domains.

name: Aurora Apartments
neighborhood: North Bethesda (Pike District) / Rockville postal area
notes: 18-story LEED-certified high-rise built 2015; 341 units; on-site Starbucks, golf simulator, rooftop terrace, dog park, bike storage.
Concierge confirmed on-site; specific 24-hour staffing not documented. Management office hours posted (Mon/Wed-Fri 9-6, Sat 10-5, Sun 12-5, Tue closed) suggest coverage is not round-the-clock.
Select units; not universal.
Cats and dogs allowed; max 75 lbs each; limit 1 pet per unit; $75/mo pet rent; breed restrictions include pit bulls, German Shepherds, Huskies.
Package receiving confirmed as listed amenity.
Individual heating and AC control per unit confirmed.

Marketing copy calls it award-winning with pristine grounds. Resident reviews are mixed: some praise attentive staff; others cite elevator-hall lighting out for extended periods and slow maintenance responses. Insufficient independent evidence to confirm or deny.
Single rental building under central management (not individually owned condos).

name: Bell at the Pike
address: 11550 Old Georgetown Road, North Bethesda, MD 20852

24-hour front desk/concierge confirmed across multiple sources

Balcony confirmed as unit amenity; described as private balcony or patio
Pool listed in some aggregator summaries but not in per-listing amenity details; not confirmed
Fitness center with yoga room confirmed; dog grooming area and pet park on site

name: Blair House
notes: 24-hour concierge confirmed on RentCafe and official blairapartments.com listing
24-hour security patrol also listed
Laundry facilities on every floor, NOT in-unit per RentCafe; some listings mention washer/dryer connections so individual units may vary
No pool found in any amenity list across multiple sources
Elevator confirmed by web search aggregation (high-rise building)
Balcony available on select units, confirmed across multiple listing sources
Pets allowed: dogs and cats, max 2 at 100 lbs each, $350 deposit + $45/month per pet, breed restrictions apply
Package lockers confirmed
Fitness center with yoga room confirmed

Part of the Blairs district, LEED platinum certified, managed by Tower Construction Group

name: Central
notes: 243-unit mixed-use apartment building; 21,500 sq ft ground-floor retail
24-hour concierge confirmed across multiple listing sources
Dogs and cats allowed; dog grooming area and pet spa on site; cat deposit $300 + $30/mo
24-hour gym; outdoor pool; rooftop lounge
Package receiving confirmed
In-unit A/C listed as unit amenity, implying resident-controlled climate
Building opened as new construction (no substantial maintenance review data found)
Sources: apartmentlist.com, apartmentadvisor.com, apartmentfinder.com, web search aggregates June 2026

name: Cole Spring Plaza
notes: Multiple independent listing sources (RentCafe, ApartmentList, PadMapper, Zumper) consistently describe a '24-hour front desk' plus concierge service. Leasing office hours are Mon-Fri 9am-6pm, Sat 10am-5pm; front desk is separately confirmed as 24-hour.

PadMapper and Zumper describe a 'neighboring swimming pool,' implying the pool is adjacent but possibly shared or external. One Zumper review noted the pool was closed both summers the reviewer lived there. RentCafe does not list pool as an on-site amenity. Honest call is Unclear pending direct confirmation.
All sources consistently list shared on-site laundry only; no in-unit washer/dryer mentioned anywhere.
Private patios/balconies confirmed as a unit feature across RentCafe, ApartmentList, PadMapper, and Zumper. Availability may vary by unit and floor.
Cats only; up to 2 per household; $250 refundable deposit per cat; $25/month pet rent per cat. No dogs mentioned as permitted in any source.

Air conditioning is listed as a unit amenity. However, 'most utilities included in rent' (per RentCafe) suggests heat may be building-supplied. Unit-level control over both heat and AC not confirmed; marked Unclear.

RentCafe shows a 4.0/5 rating (10 reviews) with residents praising 'professionalism and care' over 25+ years and describing 'top-notch management and maintenance.' ApartmentList reviews cite cleanliness. Built 1967, 10-12 stories, 273 units.
2BR floor plans confirmed (1,348 sq ft, 2BA). No 1BR+den floor plan identified; sources list studio, 1BR, and 2BR only.
Residential rental apartment building managed by Ross Companies. Not a condo ownership building.
Package receiving/package service confirmed across RentCafe, Zumper, and search aggregators.
Fitness center confirmed across RentCafe, ApartmentList, and Zumper photos.

name: Colesville Towers
notes: Explicitly described as '24-hour attended front desk' across RentCafe, PadMapper, Zumper, and the building's own search-result snippet. Treated as Confirmed 24h.

Heat and AC are controlled on a building-wide seasonal schedule tied to county requirements: AC runs mid-May through early October, heat from October through April. Residents cannot switch independently. Marked No (building-controlled).
All listing sources describe an on-site laundry center (shared/common area). No source confirms in-unit washer/dryer. Some review snippets note machines are sometimes out of order.
Listed as 'large patio/balcony' at the unit level across multiple sources, described as a standard feature with city and park views.

Mixed reviews: some residents praise prompt maintenance (issues resolved within 24 hours); others cite aging AC/pipe systems and grounds that appear neglected. ApartmentRatings overall ~4.1/5 from 385+ reviews; maintenance sub-rating ~3.7/5. Marked Unclear.
Package service is listed as a building amenity. Front desk handles packages; some resident reviews note occasional theft concerns and lack of floor-level security cameras.
Cats and dogs allowed, max 2 pets per unit; $250 refundable deposit + $25/month per pet. Breed restrictions: pit bulls, rottweilers, chows prohibited. No weight limit.
2BR/1BA and 2BR/2BA floor plans confirmed. No 1BR+den plan identified in any source.

Mid-rise apartment, 11 stories, ~260-275 units, built 1966. Managed by ROSS Companies (Ross Management Services). Most utilities included in rent. Garage and street parking. Additional amenities: bike storage, billiards room, grilling area, sun deck, business center, his-and-hers saunas noted in some sources.
(833) 358-4212 (RentCafe listing); ext. 4685 on the (844) number per Zumper
leasing.colesville@rossmgtservices.com
As of June 2026: 1 month free on select units; $1,000 off if you apply within 72 hours of touring (move-in by 06/30/2026)

name: Eleven55 Ripley
notes: UDR property page, UDR amenities page, UDR community FAQ, ApartmentList, VeryApt resident reviews
UDR FAQ states concierge is 'open 24 hours every day.' Resident reviews are mixed: some praise front desk screening, others note inconsistent enforcement.
Rooftop pool on Sky Deck; open 11am to 9pm daily; closed September through April; unheated.
Fitness center with yoga room; open 24 hours.
Parcel Pending lockers; 24/7 resident access; $20 setup fee + $8/month.

Listed as 'Patio/Balcony' unit amenity on ApartmentList; resident reviews mention private balconies with DMV views. UDR's own amenity page does not list private balconies explicitly; not available in all units.

Multiple resident reviews cite elevator outages (4 elevators with frequent 1+ down at a time), a multi-month roach infestation, and a long-term resident summary that 'the building has declined on everything.' VeryApt overall rating 6.3/10.
Cats and dogs allowed; specific weight/breed limits not confirmed from sources reviewed.
Individual air conditioning and ceiling fans listed as in-unit amenities, indicating resident-controlled climate.
Adjacent to Silver Spring Metro Station (Red Line)

name: Fenestra at The Square
neighborhood: Rockville Town Square, Rockville, MD (near Pike & Rose / North Bethesda)

name: Galvan at Twinbrook
notes: RAM (acquired from JBG SMITH); modern industrial mixed-use building above Safeway grocery; directly across from Twinbrook Metro (Red Line)

Concierge confirmed as named amenity with named staff (Manny, Dawn, Ethan, Linda cited in reviews). Reviewer reports indicate evening-only shifts (~5-11 PM for concierge); leasing office Mon-Fri 9am-6pm, Sat 10am-5pm, Sun 12pm-5pm. No evidence of 24-hour staffing. One review noted management did not renew concierge contract. Classified as Daytime only pending confirmation from building directly.
Juliet balconies listed in marketing copy for select units; patio/balcony listed as in-unit amenity across listings
Package receiving confirmed as listed amenity across multiple sources

No source specifies whether HVAC is unit-controlled or building-controlled. Multiple listings list air conditioning as an amenity but do not distinguish central building system from in-unit thermostats. Marked Unclear.
Elevator confirmed; reviewers note it goes out periodically, sometimes for 2+ weeks at a time
Cats and dogs welcome; specific fees and weight limits not found in scraped sources

Overall rating approximately 4.2/5 on maintenance surveys. Positive reviews cite prompt same-day or next-day maintenance response. Negative reviews cite recurring A/C disputes, elevator outages lasting weeks, and management quality decline after acquisition by RAM. Mixed picture; Unclear is the honest read.
Directly across from Twinbrook Metro Station (Red Line); Safeway in building
No parking available on-site
3-15 month lease terms
3x monthly rent in household income

name: Grosvenor Park I
notes: 16-story condominium tower built 1964; 398 units; individually owned units rented by owners (not centrally managed rental building). Part of the larger Grosvenor Park complex. Walking distance to Grosvenor-Strathmore Metro (Red Line). On-site Grosvenor Market grocery store.

Condo: units are individually owned and rented privately. No single leasing office; listings appear on Zillow, Apartments.com, and through individual agents. The Rockville Real Estate Exchange site aggregates available rentals.

Multiple sources confirm 24/7 staffed front desk plus overnight security patrols 8pm-4am. The Zumper listing and RRREX both cite '24/7 concierge.' C21 listing confirms '24/7 concierge, overnight security (10 p.m.-6 a.m.).'

Present in many units but not universal. Multiple active listings explicitly mention balconies (e.g., unit 1009 has 'large northwest-facing balcony with sweeping sunset views'; unit 711 listed with 'balcony with water and lush greenery views'). Not guaranteed for every unit; verify by floor plan.
No in-unit washers or dryers. Building has communal laundry facilities (electronic card payment). Confirmed by Rockville Real Estate Exchange.
Cats permitted (case-by-case basis; some listings add fees). Dogs not allowed. Cage pets permitted within reason. Confirmed by multiple sources.

HOA condo fees for owners explicitly cover air conditioning, electricity, heat, gas, water, and sewer. For renters, whether utilities are bundled into rent depends on the individual owner-landlord. Many rental listings advertise 'all utilities included.' Whether the building controls HVAC centrally or each unit has individual control is not documented in available sources.

4.1-star aggregate rating (Birdeye, 73 reviews; sourced mostly from Google). C21 listing and Howard Hanna descriptions present renovated units with updated kitchens and baths. One older negative Superpages review cited basement odors and noise from window AC units, but dated ~17 years ago. Insufficient recent independent resident feedback to confirm current condition confidently.
Not mentioned in any source reviewed. Building has front desk staffing 24/7, which may handle packages informally, but no dedicated package room is documented.

Outdoor pool, tennis courts, fitness center/exercise room, rooftop lounge (rentable), garden room with kitchen, bicycle storage, rooftop courtyard, small library, grilling area, additional storage units (rentable). Underground and surface parking available.

Because this is a condo building with individual landlords, rent and included utilities vary unit to unit. Listings from 2025-2026 range from ~$1,890 (2BR/2BA, utilities included) to ~$3,100. Many landlords include all utilities in rent because their HOA fees already cover them.

Balconies/patios listed as an amenity on multiple sources, but described as available in 'select homes' on some listings; not every unit guaranteed.
Listed as in-home washer/dryer on all major sources, but one source qualifies it as 'select homes.'

24-hour laundry rooms on each floor (shared); no in-unit washer/dryer. Residents pay per use.

No dedicated package room listed. 24-hour concierge handles package receipt per resident review. Marked Unclear for a dedicated room.
Management praised as responsive (VeryApt 8.2/10 overall). At least one reviewer called the building 'dated' (built 1966) and noted occasional false fire alarms from gas heating.
'24-Hour Concierge Service' stated on Southern Management and RentCafe. Search result quotes: 'each of the two buildings has 24 hour door staff' and 'concierge 24h picking up packages.'
Confirmed: 15-story tower; elevator listed in VeryApt amenities.
Central A/C listed as in-unit apartment feature on RentCafe. Unit thermostat control not explicitly confirmed.

Confirmed by resident reviews on ApartmentRatings and by the developer (Washington Property Company) site; not listed on official amenities page but multiple reviewers mention it specifically
LuxerOne 24/7 package locker service listed on official amenities page
Full-size, extra-large capacity washer and dryer in all units per official amenities page

Consistent resident reviews praise maintenance responsiveness and cleanliness of amenities

name: Solaire 8200 Dixon
notes: Multiple listing sources (Trulia, ApartmentList search snippet) cite a doorman and concierge with 24-hour concierge and package service; however, the building's own amenities page lists only 'Concierge Package Room & Lockers' without staffing hours. One review noted concierge staff quality has declined. Classified as 'Likely staffed' rather than 'Confirmed 24h' because the official site does not state 24-hour staffing explicitly.
27-story, 403-unit high-rise opened November 2022; described as Silver Spring's tallest building at time of opening.
Dogs and cats allowed; restricted breeds include pit bulls, rottweilers, and German Shepherds; $250 pet fee plus $50/month pet rent per Rentable.

4.1 stars from 52 Yelp reviews as of June 2026; reviews note visually appealing modern finishes but some mention declining concierge quality; no major maintenance complaints surfaced but sample is small.

Air conditioning listed as a unit amenity across multiple listing platforms; thermostat/HVAC type (central vs. in-unit mini-split) not confirmed from public sources, but air conditioning is in-unit rather than building-controlled based on listing language.
No 1BR+den floor plan found on ApartmentList or Rentable listings; only studio, 1BR, and 2BR configurations listed.

Air conditioning listed as an in-unit amenity; specific thermostat/HVAC control type (in-unit vs. building-controlled) not confirmed in sources, but modern luxury apartment context and in-unit AC listing indicate resident-controlled.
Majority of reviews describe the building as clean and well-kept; maintenance team responsive to requests. One source noted grounds rated below average. Overall assessment is positive.

notes: 279-unit mid-rise built 2010; 5 stories; green-certified building

Concierge listed as amenity; confirmed office/concierge hours Mon-Fri 9am-6pm, Sat 10am-5pm. No source confirms 24-hour lobby staffing. Building has controlled-access entry. 24-hour emergency maintenance is separate from front-desk staffing.
Package receiving confirmed as listed amenity
Central air conditioning listed as a unit feature; described as in-unit climate control. No indication of building-controlled HVAC or shared systems.
Cats and dogs welcome, up to 2 pets each. Breed restrictions apply for dogs.
Full-sized patios and Juliet balconies both available depending on unit and floor.

Mixed resident feedback. Some long-term residents (10+ years) praise clean amenities and responsive staff. Others cite maintenance request delays, management responsiveness issues, and safety concerns in common areas. Green-certified building with modern construction. 'Unclear' is the honest read.
1BR, 1BR+den, 2BR, and two-story loft configurations available. 2BR units range 1,022-1,175 sq ft.

K!CK fitness center with cardio theater, treadmills, eSpinner, Nautilus, Smith machine, free weights
Eco-friendly saline swimming pool with water feature

name: The Crescent
notes: Multiple sources confirm concierge service 7 days/week. No source specifies 24-hour staffing hours, so rated 'Likely staffed' rather than 'Confirmed 24h.'

14-story condominium building completed 2006/occupancy March 2007, with 143 units in Downtown Silver Spring (Fenton Village). Units are individually owned and some are rented out, so lease terms and finishes vary by owner.

Fitness center, billiard room, resident lounge, shared patio/courtyard with outdoor seating and grilling station, game room, conference/meeting room, bike storage, assigned underground garage parking. No pool.

One Yelp review from a former long-term resident cited safety concerns, cockroaches, a 15-day gas outage, and water damage. No corroborating reviews found to confirm or contradict. Building last updated on Yelp April 2026.
Pet-friendly; specific breed/weight restrictions not confirmed in reviewed sources.
Air conditioning and heat confirmed; at least one source notes heat included in rent/HOA. Individual unit control implied by standard condo arrangement.

Building is condominiums; rental units are owner-listed individually. VeryApt shows 1BR from $1,780 and 2BR from $2,190; Compass and ApartmentList show 2BR at $2,400/month. Prices reflect individual owner decisions and vary.

name: The Henri at Pike & Rose
address: 11870 Grand Park Avenue, North Bethesda, MD 20852
notes: Managed by Greystar. Leasing office is frequently understaffed per reviews; residents report difficulty reaching management and slow maintenance response requiring multiple follow-up calls.
Multiple 2024-2025 reviews flag dirty hallway carpets consistently stained, urine in elevators, and reports that cleaning crews stopped vacuuming common areas regularly, leading to insect complaints.

Uses Variable Refrigerant Flow (VRF) system billed as a usage-based utility; at least one resident reported inability to get unit below 70 degrees and management blocking an HVAC inspection. In-unit thermostat control is standard but VRF issues have been reported.

A concierge/front desk exists and receives positive mentions in reviews (staff by name); one third-party listing (VeryApt) lists '24-Hour Front Desk' as an amenity, but no official source confirms 24-hour hours. Classified as Likely staffed.
Up to 2 pets per unit, cats and dogs, 70 lb limit each, $50/mo pet rent per pet; breed restrictions apply (pit bulls, rottweilers, dobermans, chows, presa canarios, akitas, malamutes, wolf-hybrids).
12th-floor rooftop pool deck.
Studios, Jr. 1BR, 1BR, 1BR+den, 2BR (some with dens). 2BR sizes range 1,091-1,661 sq ft.

Package receiving confirmed as listed amenity
Air conditioning listed as in-unit feature; no indication of building-controlled HVAC
Dogs and cats welcome; max 2 pets; $250 refundable deposit per pet; $50/mo pet rent per pet; on-site pet salon

Mixed signals: newer building with premium-finish marketing, but reviewer complaints about cheap laminate flooring with bubbling gaps, roach and mouse sightings, and inadequate hot water. Pest control reportedly comes only once weekly. Positive reviews cite responsive maintenance staff and clean common areas. 'Unclear' is the honest read.
Walking distance to Rockville Metro Station (Red Line)
4 months free parking if move-in by 6/30/26 (as of June 2026)
1BR and 2BR two-story loft configurations available in addition to standard flats

name: The Pearl
address: 180 High Park Lane, Silver Spring, MD 20910
notes: Multiple listing sources (RentCafe, Zumper, VeryApt, ApartmentAdvisor) explicitly list '24 Hour Concierge Service.' Leasing office hours (Mon-Sat 9am-6pm, Sun 12pm-5pm) are separate from concierge staffing. Resident reviews on ApartmentRatings name specific overnight concierge staff as consistently present. Rated Confirmed 24h.

LEED-certified luxury high-rise, 14 stories, 284 units, built 2017 by The Tower Companies. First Fitwel-certified multifamily building in the world. Winner of Best New Apartment 2017 (DC/MD). Located in The Blairs District, approximately 0.4 miles from Silver Spring Metro (Red Line). Walk Score 83, Transit Score 88.

Aquaterra rooftop saltwater pool with cabanas; two-story fitness center with free weekly classes; wellness kitchen; WorkLab WiFi co-working lounge; on-site urban farm; dog park and pet spa; EV charging; Zipcar car sharing; package lockers; reservable guest suite; valet dry cleaning; bike room; residents lounge; TV lounge; onsite retail; controlled access.

Private balconies available on select units across multiple configurations: standard, long, double, and penthouse double balcony. Terraces also available on some units. Not every unit includes a balcony; presence depends on specific unit selection.

2BR floor plans confirmed: Hawthorn (2/2, 1,064-1,119 sq ft, $3,172-$3,633/mo) and Olympia (2/2, 1,124 sq ft, $3,368-$3,741/mo). No 1BR+den plan found in available sources; 1BR plans are Bayberry and Duxbury. Absence of a named den plan does not rule one out, but none confirmed.

In-unit washer/dryer confirmed across multiple listing sources (Interim Homes, VeryApt, ApartmentRatings reviews) and resident reviews. One source listed 'washer/dryer connections' only; majority of sources confirm full in-unit machines. Verify with leasing for specific unit.

Air conditioning listed as a unit feature on multiple sources. Individual thermostat control vs. building-managed chilled water system not confirmed explicitly. Rated Yes (in-unit control assumed for a luxury 2017 building with this profile, but verify with leasing to confirm independently controlled thermostats).

Cats and dogs welcome. Up to 2 pets per home. Weight limit approximately 100 lbs. Breed restrictions apply (Pit Bulls, Rottweilers, Huskies, German Shepherds listed). $500 refundable deposit per pet and $45/month pet rent per RentCafe. Dog park and pet grooming station on-site.

Rated 4.5/5 across 202 resident reviews on ApartmentRatings and 7.6/10 on Zumper. Reviewers consistently describe professional, responsive staff and 24-hour emergency maintenance. Main recurring complaint is power outages during storms.
Primary leasing phone (301) 587-3275. Secondary numbers seen: (227) 238-1838 and (844) 432-0208 ext. 8735 on RentCafe and Zumper respectively; may be call-center or third-party lines.

name: The Veridian
notes: Marketed by Equity Residential as '24-hour concierge.' Multiple listing sources and resident summaries confirm 24-hour concierge language. However, at least one older resident review reported that the East-side front desk concierge position was reduced or eliminated while the 24h marketing language continued. Current search results cite named concierge staff and positive service reviews, consistent with staffed front desk. Rated 'Likely staffed' rather than 'Confirmed 24h' because no independently verified post-2024 account confirms overnight staffing on both sides.
2BR/2BA floor plans confirmed at 1,086-1,227 sq ft. 1BR+den floor plans confirmed at 910-949 sq ft with 1 bath.

457-unit rental apartment building built 2009, managed by Equity Residential. Art Deco architecture, eco-friendly green roof, two towers soaring 15 stories, steps from Silver Spring Metro (Red Line) and MARC.

Rooftop pool and sundeck, two fitness centers with cardio theater and free weights, resident pub, conference and dining rooms, private study room, e-lounge, guest suite, on-site convenience store, controlled-access entry, covered garage parking.
Dogs and cats permitted. Up to 3 of each. Monthly pet rent: $65/dog, $50/cat. One-time deposit: $300/pet. Restricted breeds policy applies.

Building described as 'beautiful' and well-maintained in most reviews. Gym equipment flagged as worn in at least one review (broken machinery, tears in wall/rug). Maintenance response times mixed; some residents report slow follow-up without communication. Rated 'Unclear' pending more recent consistent data.
Units near WMATA/CSX tracks experience train noise; residents report adapting. Interior building described as 'pretty quiet' with 'decently thick' walls.

name: Twin Towers
notes: Southern Management Corporation
Up to 2 pets; dogs up to 80 lbs each; $25/month pet rent per pet
Shared coin-op laundry rooms in basement (two per tower); no in-unit W/D confirmed by features page and resident reviews despite a data error on the main marketing page
Single-duct building HVAC converted seasonally (cooling in summer, heat in winter); residents cannot control both modes independently; some reviews note poor AC performance
ApartmentList lists patio/balcony as a unit feature; official Southern Management site and RentCafe do not mention balconies; unconfirmed
Reviews split: majority call building reasonably clean and well-maintained with responsive staff; some describe amenities as outdated
All utilities included in rent
Adjacent to Silver Spring Metro (Red Line); Walk Score 99
Studio ~450 sqft, 1BR ~550 sqft, 2BR ~1,250 sqft
